pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and export_result.sh both reference 'demodb' database; seed_demodb.py creates database 'demodb' via api_call POST to /database/demodb/plocal
task_utils.sh line 6 sets ORIENTDB_AUTH='root:${ORIENTDB_ROOT_PASS}'; seed_demodb.py line 15 encodes 'root:${ROOT_PASS}' in auth header
task_utils.sh line 4 hardcodes ORIENTDB_ROOT_PASS='GymAnything123!'; seed_demodb.py line 13 hardcodes ROOT_PASS='GymAnything123!'

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic database named 'demodb' (not real data). All test data is programmatically generated by seed_demodb.py and setup_task.sh. The OrientDB server uses root credentials (user: 'root', password: 'GymAnything123!') hardcoded in task_utils.sh and seed_demodb.py. The expected output file is '/home/ga/local_leaders.json'. The task creates a specific graph topology with test profiles (john.smith@example.com, maria.garcia@example.com, luca.rossi@example.com, anna.mueller@example.com, james.brown@example.com, etc.) to deterministically verify the local leaders logic. Ground truth expectations: John (3 friends) = leader; Anna (1 friend, with 0-friend Yuki) = leader; Luca (1 friend, with 1-friend Anna) = not leader; others with 0 outgoing friends = not leaders.
